edges.description: Reduced chloride conductance increases repetitive muscle fiber firing.
Delayed relaxation is the direct clinical expression of membrane hyperexcitability.
Repetitive muscle firing produces stiffness, especially after rest.

nodes.description: Hyperexcitable muscle fibers produce stiffness that is often worst after rest and improves with repeated movement.

meta.association: Causal dominant or recessive pathogenic variant causing myotonia congenita

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and Genetic Spectrum of Myotonia Congenita in Turkish Children.
evidence.snippet: Myotonia congenita is the most common form of nondystrophic myotonia and is caused by Mendelian inherited mutations in the CLCN1 gene encoding the voltage-gated chloride channel of skeletal muscle.

nodes.description: Pathogenic CLCN1 variants reduce skeletal muscle chloride channel function and impair stabilization of the resting membrane potential.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and Genetic Spectrum of Myotonia Congenita in Turkish Children.
evidence.snippet: Myotonia congenita is the most common form of nondystrophic myotonia and is caused by Mendelian inherited mutations in the CLCN1 gene encoding the voltage-gated chloride channel of skeletal muscle.

nodes.description: Carbamazepine is also used for symptomatic control of myotonia in some patients.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and Genetic Spectrum of Myotonia Congenita in Turkish Children.
evidence.snippet: Carbamazepine (46.3%), mexiletine (27.8%), phenytoin (9.3%) were preferred for treatment.

nodes.description: Persistent muscle firing after contraction produces clinically evident myotonia.
Lamotrigine is an alternative sodium-channel blocker for symptomatic reduction of myotonia and is particularly relevant when mexiletine is unavailable, poorly tolerated, or not preferred.

evidence.reference_title: The antimyotonic effect of lamotrigine in non-dystrophic myotonias: a double-blind randomized study.
evidence.snippet: Lamotrigine effectively reduced myotonia, emphasized by consistency between effects on patient-related outcomes and objective outcomes.

evidence.reference_title: The antimyotonic effect of lamotrigine in non-dystrophic myotonias: a double-blind randomized study.
evidence.snippet: we suggest that lamotrigine should be used as the first line of treatment for myotonia in treatment-naive patients with non-dystrophic myotonias.

evidence.reference_title: Mexiletine versus lamotrigine in non-dystrophic myotonias: a randomised, double-blind, head-to-head, crossover, non-inferiority, phase 3 trial.
evidence.snippet: Lamotrigine is an important treatment consideration in non-dystrophic myotonias alongside mexiletine

nodes.description: Sodium-channel blocking therapy with mexiletine has randomized-trial and aggregated N-of-1 evidence for reducing stiffness in nondystrophic myotonia. Cardiac contraindications and QTc/conduction risk should be reviewed before and during treatment.

evidence.snippet: Muscle stiffness may respond to sodium channel blockers such as mexiletine (currently the medication with best documented effect)

evidence.reference_title: Mexiletine for symptoms and signs of myotonia in nondystrophic myotonia: a randomized controlled trial.
evidence.snippet: Mexiletine was associated with significantly improved stiffness as reported on the IVR in both treatment periods.

evidence.reference_title: Effect of Mexiletine on Muscle Stiffness in Patients With Nondystrophic Myotonia Evaluated Using Aggregated N-of-1 Trials.
evidence.snippet: mexiletine resulted in a 100% posterior probability of reaching a clinically meaningful reduction in self-reported muscle stiffness for the nondystrophic myotonia group overall and the CLCN1 genotype subgroup

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and Genetic Spectrum of Myotonia Congenita in Turkish Children.
evidence.snippet: Carbamazepine (46.3%), mexiletine (27.8%), phenytoin (9.3%) were preferred for treatment.

nodes.description: Stiffness is often most prominent after rest and improves with repeated movement.

evidence.snippet: Myotonia congenita is characterized by muscle stiffness present from childhood

nodes.description: Transient weakness after rest and minor progressive distal weakness are especially associated with the recessive Becker form.

evidence.snippet: progressive minor distal weakness and attacks of transient weakness brought on by movement after rest

nodes.description: Delayed relaxation after contraction is the defining clinical feature and is often improved by repeated contractions.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and Genetic Spectrum of Myotonia Congenita in Turkish Children.
evidence.snippet: Myotonia congenita is the most common form of nondystrophic myotonia and is caused by Mendelian inherited mutations in the CLCN1 gene encoding the voltage-gated chloride channel of skeletal muscle.
Stiffness is relieved by repeated contractions of the muscle (the "warm-up" phenomenon).

nodes.description: Stretching, conditioning, and practical mobility strategies help patients manage stiffness and weakness.
Hyperexcitable muscle fibers continue to discharge after voluntary contraction or percussion.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and Genetic Spectrum of Myotonia Congenita in Turkish Children.
evidence.snippet: Myotonia congenita is the most common form of nondystrophic myotonia and is caused by Mendelian inherited mutations in the CLCN1 gene encoding the voltage-gated chloride channel of skeletal muscle.
